content:
First, set up coordinates.  Let  
A=(0,0),  
B=(b,0),  
C=(0,b),  
so that AB=AC=b and ∠A=90°.  

Then  
D lies on AB with AD=48√2 ⇒ D=(48√2,0),  
E lies on AC with AE=52√2 ⇒ E=(0,52√2).  

The line BC has equation x+y=b.  To project a point P=(x₀,y₀) onto x+y=b, one subtracts the signed distance \(\frac{x₀+y₀-b}{\sqrt2}\) in the direction of the unit normal \(\frac{(1,1)}{\sqrt2}\).  

1) From D=(d,0) with d=48√2:  
 signed distance = \(\frac{d+0-b}{\sqrt2}=\frac{d-b}{\sqrt2}\).  
 Foot X = D – (signed dist)·(1/√2,1/√2)  
   = \(\bigl(d,0\bigr)\;-\;\frac{d-b}{2}\,(1,1)\)  
   = \(\Bigl(\frac{b+d}2,\;\frac{b-d}2\Bigr).\)  

2) From E=(0,e) with e=52√2:  
 signed distance = \(\frac{0+e-b}{\sqrt2}=\frac{e-b}{\sqrt2}\).  
 Foot Y = E – (signed dist)·(1/√2,1/√2)  
   = \((0,e)\;-\;\frac{e-b}{2}(1,1)\)  
   = \(\Bigl(\frac{b-e}2,\;\frac{b+e}2\Bigr).\)  

Then  
Δx = Xₓ–Yₓ = \(\frac{b+d}2-\frac{b-e}2=\frac{d+e}2,\)  
Δy = Xᵧ–Yᵧ = \(\frac{b-d}2-\frac{b+e}2=-\,\frac{d+e}2.\)  

Hence  
\(XY=\sqrt{\bigl(\tfrac{d+e}2\bigr)^2+\bigl(-\tfrac{d+e}2\bigr)^2}
=\frac{d+e}{2}\sqrt2
=\frac{48\sqrt2+52\sqrt2}2\;\sqrt2
=100.\)

Answer: \boxed{100}

First, set up coordinates.  Let  
A=(0,0),  
B=(b,0),  
C=(0,b),  
so that AB=AC=b and ∠A=90°.  

Then  
D lies on AB with AD=48√2 ⇒ D=(48√2,0),  
E lies on AC with AE=52√2 ⇒ E=(0,52√2).  

The line BC has equation x+y=b.  To project a point P=(x₀,y₀) onto x+y=b, one subtracts the signed distance \(\frac{x₀+y₀-b}{\sqrt2}\) in the direction of the unit normal \(\frac{(1,1)}{\sqrt2}\).  

1) From D=(d,0) with d=48√2:  
 signed distance = \(\frac{d+0-b}{\sqrt2}=\frac{d-b}{\sqrt2}\).  
 Foot X = D – (signed dist)·(1/√2,1/√2)  
   = \(\bigl(d,0\bigr)\;-\;\frac{d-b}{2}\,(1,1)\)  
   = \(\Bigl(\frac{b+d}2,\;\frac{b-d}2\Bigr).\)  

2) From E=(0,e) with e=52√2:  
 signed distance = \(\frac{0+e-b}{\sqrt2}=\frac{e-b}{\sqrt2}\).  
 Foot Y = E – (signed dist)·(1/√2,1/√2)  
   = \((0,e)\;-\;\frac{e-b}{2}(1,1)\)

Δx = Xₓ–Yₓ = \(\frac{b+d}2-\frac{b-e}2=\frac{d+e}2,\)

=\frac{d+e}{2}\sqrt2
=\frac{48\sqrt2+52\sqrt2}2\;\sqrt2
=100.\)

Answer: \boxed{100}
